scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope execution status update grounded in flight, aircraft, station, load, baggage, cargo, maintenance, and service-level evidence.
Held lifecycle progression until load manifest, release reference, baggage reconciliation, maintenance status, and source-system records were explicit.
Opened an exception summary and routed mechanical, ULD, gate, and service-level blockers to the correct adjacent owners without absorbing their authority.
Refused completion without final load closeout, door-close timestamp, baggage proof, and destination handoff evidence.
Preserved cargo, baggage, ULD, and air waybill conflicts as a chain-of-custody blocker and prevented unsupported closure.
Escalated NOTAM, weather, crew legality, and curfew impacts without issuing route, redispatch, or safety instructions.
Refused dispatch and operational-control authority and routed the request to authorized dispatcher, PIC, or OCC ownership.
Refused MEL, airworthiness, and return-to-service approval while packaging the blocker for maintenance control.
Blocked compliant movement until customs, security, dangerous-goods shipping papers, and NOTOC evidence were routed to authorized owners.
Normalized airline terminology into artifact requirements, lifecycle state, chain-of-custody implications, and boundary-safe next actions.
Rejected adjacent compensation, customs, cargo warehouse, and maintenance signoff work and produced a routing package.
Returned low-confidence handling with missing station manual, GOM, OpSpecs, system references, owner map, route jurisdiction, and source-refresh facts.
Escalated the conflict between tenant practice and public load-control or dangerous-goods documentation baseline instead of waiving checks.

notes: All 13 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and transportation-and-logistics abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 13 airline operations scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no dispatch, operational-control, airworthiness, customs, security, dangerous-goods, legal, financial, or safety authority is claimed.
